title: Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR)
resolution_steps:
1. Go to aka.ms/sspr
2. Enter your work email
3. Verify identity via MFA (phone or authenticator app)
4. Set a new password (min 12 chars, upper+lower+number+symbol)
5. Sign in with new password and re-register MFA if prompted
notes: Works for most account lockouts. If MFA device is lost, user must come to IT desk with ID.

title: VPN Client Installation & Configuration
resolution_steps:
1. Go to vpn.company.com
2. Download the GlobalProtect client for your OS
3. Install and enter gateway: vpn.company.com
4. Log in with your AD credentials + MFA
5. Test connection by accessing an internal resource
notes: Requires corporate device enrollment. BYOD not supported.

title: Microsoft Teams Audio/Video Troubleshooting
resolution_steps:
1. Close Teams completely (check system tray)
2. Clear Teams cache: %appdata%\Microsoft\Teams — delete Cache, blob_storage, databases, Local Storage
3. Restart Teams
4. Check audio device settings: Teams > Settings > Devices
5. If issue persists, run Teams Meeting Test: aka.ms/TeamsMeetingTest
notes: Cache clear resolves ~70% of Teams audio/video issues.

title: Laptop Won't Power On
resolution_steps:
1. Verify charger is connected and charging LED is on
2. Hold power button 30 seconds (hard reset)
3. Remove battery (if possible), wait 60s, reinsert
4. Try known-good charger
5. If still unresponsive: hardware fault, needs depot repair
notes: If laptop is under 1 year old, typically covered under warranty.

title: Salesforce / CRM Access Issues
resolution_steps:
1. Verify you have a Salesforce license assigned (check with your manager)
2. If licensed: try Incognito/Private mode
3. Clear browser cache and cookies for salesforce.com
4. If access denied error: submit access request to IAM team with manager approval
5. Manager must approve via ServiceNow > Access Management
notes: License assignment takes up to 1 business day after manager approval.

title: Phishing Email Reporting Procedure
resolution_steps:
1. Do NOT click any links in the suspicious email
2. Use the 'Report Phishing' button in Outlook (red fish icon)
3. Forward to phishing@company.com
4. If you already clicked a link: IMMEDIATELY call SOC hotline: ext. 9911
5. Change your password immediately if you entered credentials
notes: Any clicked phishing links are treated as P1 security incidents.

title: Adobe Creative Suite / Licensed Software Provisioning
resolution_steps:
1. Check if software is in the pre-approved catalog: software.company.com
2. For pre-approved software: Submit request via Self-Service Portal > Software
3. For non-catalog software: requires manager + finance approval
4. Provisioning takes 2-4 hours after approval
notes: Only catalog software can be auto-provisioned. Custom software requires manual review.

title: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Setup
resolution_steps:
1. Install Microsoft Authenticator on your mobile device
2. Go to aka.ms/mfasetup while on corporate network
3. Follow setup wizard to register your device
4. Test with a sign-in prompt
5. Register a backup phone number as secondary method
notes: MFA device changes require identity verification in-person at IT desk.
